violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the basement, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The grease receptacle outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025
The ceiling in the basement & front cook like area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
Canned pumpkin was observed adulterated by rodent droppings on lids. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The door to the outside, located in the basement, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of rodent droppings and other debris. 1) Dishwasher 2) Refrigerator (basement) 3) Freezer 4) Ledge 5) Grill table (basement) 5) Shelving (warewashing area) New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The grease receptacle outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
Inspector observed missing ceiling tiles in the basement prep area. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
The ceiling in the front cook line and basement ware-washing areas are covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The cutting board of the small flip top prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
The plumbing under the hand wash sink in the basement are was in poor repair (leak observed). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The ceiling in the front cook line and basement ware-washing areas are covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC instructed to remove dish machine from service and switch to using three bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

Tomatoes and liquid eggs were observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved items to another cold hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The small flip top prep unit in the front cooking area was in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature). PIC instructed to remove all foods from unit and remove from service. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The cutting board on the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. PIC instructed to no longer use this surface for cutting. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) Racks in refrigeration unit had biofilm 2) Fan covers in refrigeration unit had dust and biofilm New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
The plumbing under the hand wash sink in the basement area was in poor repair (leak observed). New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
The ceiling in the front cook line and basement ware-washing areas are covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
